title: Inject shared memory environment into agent runners
description: Update runner environment construction for all supported backends so agent subprocesses receive `TAKT_CMD`, `AGENT_MEMORY_DB`, and `AGENT_TAKT_FEATURE_ROOT_ID`, with self-hosting-aware takt command resolution.
acceptance_criteria: Both runner backends inject the three required environment variables.
`TAKT_CMD` resolution follows the self-hosting/global-install fallback rules in the spec.
The injected DB path always points at the main project `.takt/memory/memory.db` rather than a worktree-local location.

handoff_summary.completed: Added `import shutil`. Added `_find_project_root(start)` that walks up from workdir to locate the `.takt` directory — this ensures the DB path always resolves to the main project root, not a worktree-local copy. Added `_resolve_takt_cmd(workspace_root)` implementing the three-level fallback: self-hosting (pyproject.toml contains "agent-takt") → `uv run --directory <root> takt`; global install → absolute PATH binary; last resort → uv run form. Updated both `CodexAgentRunner.run_bead()` and `ClaudeCodeAgentRunner.run_bead()` to build a `memory_env` dict with the three required vars at the start of each call, then merge any caller-supplied `execution_env` on top (preserving the ability for tests or special cases to override). Set `AGENT_TAKT_FEATURE_ROOT_ID` to `bead.feature_root_id or "global"` so standalone beads receive the literal string "global".
handoff_summary.risks: Downstream bead B-2b4f2514 (update memory skill content and guardrail templates) depends on both this bead and B-f02faf8f. No file conflicts expected — B-2b4f2514 touches template files and SKILL.md files, not runner.py.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester should verify env injection in both runner backends, the three fallback cases of _resolve_takt_cmd, the standalone-bead global value, and that the DB path resolves to the main project root even when workdir is a nested worktree or exec_root/repo path.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — only runner.py modified; no overlap with sibling beads' expected files.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: _find_project_root walks upward from workdir checking for a .takt subdirectory. This is robust across all call sites: exec_root/repo (4 levels inside .takt), worktree paths (3 levels inside .takt), and Path.cwd() from propose_plan. No runner API changes (no new constructor parameter) were needed because the project root can always be derived from workdir. The memory_env dict is built first and then updated with caller-provided execution_env so that explicit overrides take precedence — this is important for test harnesses that need to inject specific values. The _resolve_takt_cmd function reads pyproject.toml text rather than parsing TOML, which is intentional: the check only needs to detect presence of the string "agent-takt" and avoids an additional dependency on a TOML parser.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Test cases needed: (1) _find_project_root with a path nested inside .takt — assert returns the directory one level above .takt; (2) _find_project_root with no .takt ancestor — assert returns start path; (3) _resolve_takt_cmd with a pyproject.toml containing "agent-takt" — assert returns uv run form; (4) _resolve_takt_cmd without agent-takt in pyproject.toml but takt on PATH — assert returns the binary path; (5) _resolve_takt_cmd with no pyproject.toml and no takt binary — assert returns uv run form; (6) CodexAgentRunner.run_bead with a mock bead having feature_root_id — assert subprocess env contains all three vars with correct values; (7) ClaudeCodeAgentRunner.run_bead same; (8) standalone bead (feature_root_id=None) — assert AGENT_TAKT_FEATURE_ROOT_ID="global"; (9) caller-supplied execution_env overrides memory_env defaults.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: N/A — implementation is complete as specified. The memory.db file itself is not created by this bead; that is handled by the onboarding/memory-init bead (B-f02faf8f). The env vars will be injected even before the DB exists, which is correct — agents that run before init has been called will receive the path but the file won't be there; this is an acceptable bootstrapping constraint documented in the spec.

conflict_risks: None — only runner.py modified; no overlap with sibling beads' expected files.

execution_history.summary: Injected TAKT_CMD, AGENT_MEMORY_DB, and AGENT_TAKT_FEATURE_ROOT_ID into the subprocess environment for both CodexAgentRunner and ClaudeCodeAgentRunner. Added _find_project_root() and _resolve_takt_cmd() module-level helpers following the spec's exact logic.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
